title: narcissus linnaeanus subsp. ganymedoides
intro: Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. ganymedoides, commonly known as the Ganymede narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and strap-shaped leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the Ganymede narcissus has been recognized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of the Ganymede narcissus are often dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
